title: Create safe fallback point-size path
overview: Introduce a uniform-guarded fallback so that when the per-vertex `size` attribute is absent, shaders fall back to a uniform base point size instead of producing zero-size (invisible) or broken points. A new `uHasSizeAttr` uniform (0.0/1.0) controls whether the `size` attribute is read; when 0.0, a multiplier of 1.0 is used instead. The attribute registry is updated to classify `size` as optional, and geometry systems set the flag when they attach the attribute.

files_to_create.purpose: Dedicated test file covering all four acceptance criteria: default point-size path without size attribute, uniform base size rendering, conditional size modulation, and shader compilation without optional size attributes.

files_to_modify.changes: Add `uniform float uHasSizeAttr;` declaration. Replace `size` in point-size calculation with `mix(1.0, size, uHasSizeAttr)` so that when uHasSizeAttr is 0.0, the multiplier is 1.0 (uniform base size only).

Add `{ name: 'uHasSizeAttr', type: 'float', defaultValue: 0.0 }` to COMMON_UNIFORMS. Add OPTIONAL_POINTCLOUD_ATTRIBUTES, OPTIONAL_PARTICLEFIELD_ATTRIBUTES, and OPTIONAL_RIBBONFIELD_ATTRIBUTES arrays that each contain `{ name: 'size', itemSize: 1 }`. Remove `size` from the required attribute arrays.

After attaching the `size` BufferAttribute, set `material.uniforms.uHasSizeAttr.value = 1.0`. Add `uHasSizeAttr` to the uniforms block with default 0.0. Update REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES reference to exclude `size` (now optional).
Same changes as pointCloud.ts — add uHasSizeAttr uniform, set to 1.0 when size attribute is attached.
Same changes as pointCloud.ts — add uHasSizeAttr uniform, set to 1.0 when size attribute is attached.
Same changes as pointCloud.ts — add uHasSizeAttr uniform, set to 1.0 when size attribute is attached (if this system exists and uses a size attribute).

Update validateGeometryAttributes to accept an optional `optionalAttrs` parameter. Optional attributes are validated for correctness (itemSize, finite values) only when present, but their absence does not produce an error.
No structural changes needed — AttributeSpec already supports the optional attribute pattern.

steps.title: Add uHasSizeAttr uniform to shader registry
steps.description: Add `{ name: 'uHasSizeAttr', type: 'float', defaultValue: 0.0 }` to COMMON_UNIFORMS. Move `{ name: 'size', itemSize: 1 }` from each required attributes array into new exported optional attribute arrays: OPTIONAL_POINTCLOUD_ATTRIBUTES, OPTIONAL_PARTICLEFIELD_ATTRIBUTES, OPTIONAL_RIBBONFIELD_ATTRIBUTES. The required arrays retain position, color (where applicable), aHueOffset, and aRandom.
steps.acceptance: TypeScript compiles. Required attribute arrays no longer contain size. Optional arrays export correctly.

steps.title: Update all four vertex shaders with guarded point-size path
steps.description: In each of the four vertex shaders (pointWarp, particleWarp, crystalWarp, ribbonWarp): (1) Add `uniform float uHasSizeAttr;` after the other uniform declarations. (2) Replace the line `float pointSize = size * uBasePointSize * (2200.0 / depth) * trebleSparkle;` with `float sizeMultiplier = mix(1.0, size, uHasSizeAttr);` followed by `float pointSize = sizeMultiplier * uBasePointSize * (2200.0 / depth) * trebleSparkle;`. The `mix(1.0, size, 0.0)` yields 1.0 when the attribute is absent (safe baseline); `mix(1.0, size, 1.0)` yields the per-vertex size when present.
steps.acceptance: Each shader has the new uniform declared and uses sizeMultiplier instead of raw size in the gl_PointSize computation. No other shader logic is changed.

steps.title: Update geometry systems to declare and set uHasSizeAttr
steps.description: In each geometry system: (1) Add `uHasSizeAttr: { value: 0.0 }` to the ShaderMaterial uniforms block. (2) After the `geometry.setAttribute('size', ...)` call, set `material.uniforms.uHasSizeAttr.value = 1.0;`. (3) Update the REQUIRED_ATTRIBUTES import/reference to use the new required-only array from shaderRegistry (without size). This ensures the uniform defaults to safe (0.0) and is only enabled when the attribute is actually attached.
steps.acceptance: Each system compiles, sets uHasSizeAttr to 1.0 after attaching size, and references the updated required attributes array.

steps.title: Update geometryValidator for optional attributes
steps.description: Add an optional second parameter `optionalAttrs?: AttributeSpec[]` to validateGeometryAttributes. For each optional attribute: if it exists on the geometry, validate its itemSize and finite values (same checks as required); if it does not exist, skip without error. Update the return type/errors to distinguish optional validation failures if needed. Update geometry system validation calls to pass the optional attributes array.
steps.acceptance: Validator passes when optional attributes are missing. Validator catches itemSize or NaN errors in optional attributes when they are present. Existing required attribute validation is unchanged.

steps.title: Write tests for fallback point-size behavior
steps.description: Create test cases covering: (T-051-01) Shader GLSL source includes uHasSizeAttr uniform and the mix() fallback pattern in all four vertex shaders. (T-051-02) Geometry systems set uHasSizeAttr to 1.0 when size attribute is present — init a system and inspect uniform value. (T-051-03) When size attribute is removed from geometry after init and uHasSizeAttr is set to 0.0, the shader still has a valid point-size path (sizeMultiplier = 1.0). (T-051-04) Geometry validator passes when optional size attribute is absent. (T-051-05) Geometry validator catches invalid optional size attribute (wrong itemSize, NaN values) when present. (T-051-06) uHasSizeAttr defaults to 0.0 in shaderRegistry COMMON_UNIFORMS. (T-051-07) Required attribute arrays no longer include size; optional arrays do.
steps.acceptance: All test cases pass via vitest.

steps.title: Update existing shader integrity tests
steps.description: Update any existing tests that assert on required attribute counts or names to reflect that `size` has moved from required to optional. Ensure T-049 tests still pass with the new registry structure. Add a test confirming uHasSizeAttr appears in the uniform spec.
steps.acceptance: All existing shader integrity tests pass. New uniform is covered.

steps.title: Build and verify
steps.description: Run `npm run build` to confirm no TypeScript errors. Run `npm run test` to confirm all tests pass (existing + new). Manual verification: load the app in browser and confirm the scene renders normally with per-point sizes active (uHasSizeAttr = 1.0). Optionally test the fallback by temporarily removing the size setAttribute call from one system to confirm uniform base size rendering.
steps.acceptance: Build passes. All tests pass. No console errors. Scene renders with visible, correctly-sized points. No shader compilation errors.

testing_strategy.unit_tests: fallbackPointSize.test.ts covers GLSL source patterns, uniform defaults, registry structure, and validator optional-attribute handling.
testing_strategy.integration_tests: Geometry system tests verify that uHasSizeAttr is set correctly after init. Shader integrity tests confirm the new uniform is declared and consistent across all shaders.
testing_strategy.manual_verification: Load the app and confirm normal rendering. Temporarily remove size attribute from one system to verify the fallback produces visible uniform-sized points instead of invisible or broken output.

risks.risk: Moving size from required to optional could cause existing US-049/US-050 tests to fail if they assert on specific required attribute counts
risks.mitigation: Step 6 explicitly updates those tests. The attribute still exists on all geometry systems — it is just validated as optional rather than required.

risks.risk: GLSL mix(1.0, size, 0.0) when size attribute is unbound — WebGL provides a default attribute value of 0.0 for unbound float attributes
risks.mitigation: mix(1.0, 0.0, 0.0) = 1.0, which is exactly the safe fallback. The mix approach is robust regardless of the unbound default value.

risks.risk: Adding a uniform to all shaders could cause Three.js material needsUpdate issues
risks.mitigation: The uniform is added in the ShaderMaterial constructor uniforms block, so it is present from first compile. No dynamic uniform addition is needed.

definition_of_done: Shader supports a valid default point-size path without optional custom size attributes
Scene renders with a uniform base point size when per-point size data is unavailable
Optional size modulation can be enabled only when corresponding attributes are present
Missing optional size attributes do not break shader compilation
No console errors on normal load
No shader compile errors or WebGL validation errors
